text: I was with Tmobile for over 10 years, but it really went downhill. Poor customer service and price kept hiking every year. No quality service or benefits (upgrades, discounts, promotions) staying with them. I tried to keep my number, but they wouldn't let me when I switched carriers. All customer support people I talked too were from India or Philippines and they weren't that helpful.

The WORST service that I’m absolutely dumbfounded. Called to get a receipt for a temporary eSIM. A receipt. 1 hour goes by and they still can’t resolve the issue, I ask to speak to the supervisor who turns out to be the absolute worst person for the job imaginable. The person’s name was AJ. After explaining my situation to him and that I’ve been on the call with them for an hour, AJ had zero apologies but instead began to argue with me over their incompetence 😂 said that their security is just SO good that they can’t access my account by any means. When I said that this experience was disappointing, he has the nerve to argue and say “no it is not” after which I hung up. So AJ if you’re reading this, hope you get fired and work someplace FAR from customers.

Signed up for T-Mobile Home Internet about 6 months ago. I was promised a $200.00 gift card for switching from Spectrum. Guess what, NO gift card. I have talked to them on the phone, text and email. All with the same answer, it was approved but not sent. It's been 2 months now. No holding my breath.

Over the last year or so T mobile service in Denver has become near non existent. I can barely make calls and if I am able to make one they drop almost immediately. My texts don’t go through and I never have internet unless I’m connected to WiFi. It doesn’t matter where I am in Denver or Colorado. I’ve been unable to contact family during emergencies and unable to carry out payments with my phone when there isn’t WiFi. I can’t imagine worse coverage. I just paid off my phone so that I could unlock it and switch. I cannot recommend enough that you do not switch to T mobile under any circumstances.
I wish I could give a -1 .

Would give them a zero!!   Tried to return because internet didn’t work in my house.  Argued for 1 hour with customer service over 1.83.  Was so tempted to just pay it.  The support representative kept telling me it was a legitimate charge but I canceled in the allotted amount of time.   Finally asked for a manager  and they were able to take the charge off.  This is a very bad way to treat customers!!

I wish I could give 0 , MISS information just to make more money , when they do get the money the response is “ we can’t do anything about it” bought a new phone “on them” and now they want me to pay it off in order to use it internationally when they told me I won’t have to ! Fkn Ridiculous

I was having a problem with my phone constantly going to "do not disturb" on its own. It wouldn't let me do anything! I finally took it to Edward at the South Haven, Michigan store and he quickly solved the problem! It hasn't given a problem since! (I had an app that took over the phone. He removed it...)

I am extremely disappointed with T-Mobile’s customer service and policies. My wife and I purchased a brand new iPhone 14, and after two days, we decided to return it unopened in exchange for an IPhone 15. To our shock, we were hit with a ridiculous $70 restocking fee. The store clerk had clearly told us that we had until Monday (three days after the purchase) to return the phone, especially since we were still deciding between the iPhone 14 and iPhone 15.
Despite this, T-Mobile refused to waive the fee. We’ve been loyal customers for years, but this experience has made us question our commitment to the company. It’s incredibly frustrating to be treated this way, especially when we followed the store’s instructions. I will seriously reconsider my options moving forward. We’ve since disputed the charge with our bank because a restocking fee is a complete gimmick designed to make more money for a multimillion-dollar company. It makes no sense to charge a restocking fee for a phone that was never opened and was returned just two days later.
T-Mobile, please do better—your long-time customers deserve more respect and transparency.
The worst company and network carrier ever.

Wish I could leave 0 stars. Worst customer service I have ever received from any company ever. From the beginning they tell you that you’ll receive this discount for such and such and so on. Lied about all of it. No discount for new line with phone purchase, no discount for watch purchase with line. Even said here try this internet for 30days free. Showed up on my bill in a week. When I called about not receiving the discounts discussed. They issued a $100  debit card and then canceled it after I received it. Finally switched service, paid my final bill and received another bill a month later. When I called it was another final bill for the watch line they didn’t cancel but charged me for. Called customer service and they said they couldn’t issue a refund but would issue a debit card. Never received it so I called to ask where it was and was told they wouldn’t be issuing one even after I asked for it to be documented. Nope. Run away!!!!!

1canelle my service after being with t mobile for over 10 years and the continued to charge me
 It event cost me and addition 375 dollars as the threatened my credit

Call to get a $7 late fee removed .because there was a discrepancy in the payment last month. and I have never been late been with the company for 12 years,and they would not do it I do not recommend this company at all

published review 12/5/24
I had T-Mobile for over 10 years and I decided to switch last April because the coverage was getting bad. After I switched I called T-Mobile customer service to unlock the phones and cancel my service. Yesterday my wife noticed they have been charging us for months and when I called to get a refund they said that I never cancelled. They have the record of me calling to unlock my phones and cancel those but they kept 1 line and charged me over $165 a month and will not refund my money. They stated that I never requested to cancel a line that I never had. When you sign up the sleazy salesman sign you up for extra lines for there promotions and do not tell you about it. My line had 9 lines and I only have 3 phones and a iPad. Run from this crappy company.

Having a business account for over six years, biggest mistake was to leave Verizon and go to T-Mobile. I always went to the Bethpage store on Broadway this past January. I got new phones handed my old phones and nothing was ever applied to my account and the promotion was also never applied to my account. I have been calling T-Mobile since February And now we are in December. Not only the corporate office is giving me the runaround and saying I’m really sorry my hands are tied so is the store. At this Bethpage store the crew constantly changes every few months, which is very fishy. My bill has more than doubled and today I received a call From the business department because again I have a business account. She put me on hold she came back on the phone and gave me the same spiel, Which was I’m really sorry I know it’s frustrating. Go to another store. You do the running around. There’s nothing we can do. And if I can give them less than zero, I would
t mobile is trash. good riddons

I can’t believe this guy from T-Mobile have the nerve to ask me when I call back. Are you calling back to join back up with T-Mobile? I said are you out of your goddamn mind I closed down my account on October 5 and I went in the store, I said I would like to pay off everything I owe so I paid $233 on October 5 a month later I receive a bill for $312 and they said this is my final bill. I know what was the last bill that I paid they’re just making up a bunch of crap we get this. My phone still has a Sim lock so I just called up today. This is six months later and even though I paid that $312 they charged interest on it I’m telling you right now stay far away from T-Mobile. They are the crook and worst company you have ever been with in your life. My company and me. I am done with Verizon Print. I feel bad that Sprint went to T-Mobile cause Sprint was decent and T-Mobile destroyed T-Mobile you guys need to go to hell you’re crooked you’re dishonest your liars and you’re the f in  tallyban this company has bin laden written all over it

On Sat. Nov. 30, I went to the T-mobile store on Twenty Mile Road, in Parker, because my phone (bought from a local retailer) wasn't holding a charge. 
I had gone to Batteries Plus earlier in the day, who said the battery was no good. 
They said a new battery would be around $80.00. The phone was old, & was purchased new for $90.00 2 years ago.  It wasn't worth replacing the battery. 
When I went to T-mobile, I was met by 2 gentleman, who couldn't have been more understanding, gracious and eager to help! 
They both worked together on my phone to get it to charge. It would charge to 15, then drop to 2 once the charging cable was removed. The same issues I was having at home. 
Kway went out of his way to find me a phone I could comfortably afford; then he worked diligently to transfer my old phone information to the new phone,  which wasn't easy, since a phone needs to reach 20% to transfer. 
With his efforts, and his amazing knowledge of cell phones, he was able to transfer all of my contacts, as well as very important apps I'd saved in the old phone! 
I've been with T-mobile since they were Voicestream (30+ years?) and again,  realized last night that I've made the correct decision to stay with them, even though MANY companies have tried to lure me away with 'cheaper' plans!
Thank you Kway AND T-Mobile....
Tmobile sucks always messing things up over charging me really bad but I think all phone companies are

I would give 0 Star if I could.  We purchased a Home Internet system - Home Internet Plus.  When we were done, they gave us 3 trackers for "FREE".  Two days later (including Thanksgiving", we went to the store to return the trackers as we didn't think we would use them.  We were told that we had to pay $20/each restock fee to return them.  Nobody even asked us if we wanted them when we purchased the system.  They just put in the bag with the  internet package, without explaining any extra possible charges, such as if we lost one and others..  We feel that it was a scam designed by the company or the sales representatives working there were not honest.

Unbelievable, I had to return a cell phone to T-Mobile and an IPad that was under warranty. The phone is fine but they sent me a different color ipad that was damaged with dents ans scratches. The representative assured me she would call me back to see if I should keep my iPad and return theirs or vice versa, she called me and said hold the iPads I am waiting for an answer but never called back now despite giving them the tracking numbers for both devices being returned they are billing me $1600 !!! Time to leave Tmobile no one can clarify the bill, a new rep is in contact with me who said she will have the old rep call me now the old rep calls back and says they can't take there own damaged replacement back!!!!

STAY AWAY!!! After more than 20 years with Sprint/T-mobile, I recently switched phone service to Spectrum about a week before the end of the billing cycle. I was immediately locked out of my online T-mobile account, yet they managed to draft another full month’s payment. I called to request the final bill which took almost another month to receive. I was billed extra for closing my account mid-cycle. So instead of getting a refund, they said I still owed yet a partial payment. I still couldn’t log in to pay the balance, request a refund or anything at all because I no longer have an account. Then once again they managed to draft my bank account for the amount they say I owe. What a terrible company!  So glad I switched to Spectrum. My phone service is so much more reliable and I am paying less than half what T-mobile was charging monthly. I will never go back to them after this.

I have been a TMobile customer for over 2 years. The prices keep going up service is not great. I have dropped calls no service in areas. Then I get hit with a free upgrade. Guess what it was not free I paid the fees then get my bill to discover I was billed $399 for the upgraded phone. I'm very  unhappy with the service. The customer service isn't much better. My prices continue to climb but I'm not getting anything else for my service. I do not recommend TMobile.  Go somewhere else.

Overall, I don't have a problem with T-Mobile. I've been with them since their beginnings. What I have a problem with is to be insulted and lectured to by the AI. I needed to change a setting on the phone, (Which I finally figured out on my own. Not going to write what the technical support said to correct it). Thank God my Do Not Disturb turned on as she was trying to tell me some convoluted thing to do. My problem was I had changed the settings from Google to Samsung for the keyboard 
and voice texting. But could't change it back. Hence both were fighting for dominance. And I was stuck with repeat text messages. At least this phone wasn't sending them out like the s23 was. This time calling T-Mobile was a complete waste of my time. However, in all the years I have had them.I really can't complain. But get rid of The person who programmed the AI!!!

I purchased a Samsung Galaxy 7 watch through T-mobile about 10 days ago. I love Samsung products because their support team will help you resolve and understand any issue. However, Samsung couldn't help me because the watch kept asking for a PUK (Sim card) number that T-mobile had to give me. Not as easy as it sounds. I called tech support and got CARTER B. who is probably the best customer service representative that I've ever dealt with in my life! At 70, that's saying lots! CARTER B. could hear my frustration, just as much as I thought he was stumped. The difference? I gave up, he didn't. He researched, and researched more, and after 2 hours said he'd call me back shortly, with an answer, and again reassured me it was all going to be fine. Through the hours he spent tirelessly on the phone with me, he never sounded angry, frustrated, sick of me whining, or anything other than supportive and dedicated. He would not let me go until I felt like I was confident in myself to understand my watch. He is amazing, patient, respectful, intelligent, kind and the best customer service representative ever! I've ran many huge corporations during my working career and I would've been honored to have him represent me/my company. I hope T-mobile realizes what a gem he is, because this young man is going to make any company where he works a success! High 5 CARTER B!!! Let's give CARTER B. the standing ovation he very much deserves!
I can't understand why this company made it so big. Incompetent workers, customer service, high prices, worst company I'm switching after 12 years with them.

Run.......The worst customer service.  Two days trying to return a defective tablet.  Nothing but the run around and talked to some many people and a different story with each one!!!  i would NEVER EVER recommed them for tablets.
Shout out to Reggie on the phone for helping me reset my phone after it stopped connecting to my cellular. Works perfect now and he was so helpful thank you REGGIE!!!!!

Are the customer reps at T-Mobile in a competition to see who can be the rudest and least helpful overall to their clients? That is the only explanation I can fathom for the continued experience I've had with the company over these past three years. On the technical side, I do receive basically reliable phone service and I can live with the bill. While all my devices are eligible for upgrade, and I want to upgrade all of our devices; T-Mobile doesn't want to reach across the aisle with any enticing offers. They are beyond greedy and show no respect for long time loyal customers.

I was a customer of t mobile for proabaly a  YEAR maybe more,Every other Month my bill would be higher ,I would have to call them talk to one of their  managers and get it straighten out it didn't matter the next bill would be fine the next bill would always be higher, the last time was the last time  I just quit them as i told them I would if it kept happening Now I found a new company  but still have the phone from t mobile,I have no use for it but refuse to pay when they keep trying to over charge me my bill was $45.00 a month not $69.00.like i said every other month .I have kept records but might have thrown them away.they keep a log of all phone calls so I can prove if I have to what they were trying to do ,my son also quit them for the same reason.now I get warning letters along with a raise in what I owe for BEING LATE.I HOPE THEY DONT HOLD THEIR BREATH  THEY ARE DONE ROBBING ME

I called to get my balance. The representative barely spoke English. He said he couldn't verify me and will not give me any information on my account. I've had the same number for 20 years. I DID understand the part where when I asked he said he was in the Philippines.
Shame on you T-Mobile that you 
outsource for the sacrifice of quality customer service. I'm looking into another carrier tomorrow!

author.name: Wee The People Preschool and C

text: Closing this account has been a nightmare.  I have been to the store three times to request that the account be closed, with each time getting a verbal confirmation, yet each month they continue to bill my account.  I have spent countless hours on the phone as well.  I finally had to close our bank account to prevent further charges.  After over $1000 of fraudulent charges, T-Mobile still refuses to provide documentation that the account has been closed, or to refund the charges.  I'm now exploring what to do in response to this fraudulent interaction.

I am a 77 yr old woman and I was having problems answering incoming calls. I went to T-mobile store in London Ohio. I received very professional assistance. They were kind and patient with me. I appreciated their service and would recommend this store highly. 614-581-4075. B. Elfrink

T-Mobile is the absolute worst!!! I just got a bill and a debt collector statement on the same day when I canceled my service on October 20. The bill was for October 10-Nov 09. Again I canceled on Oct 20. Oh and it had a $15.50 late charge even though I never got a final statement. Keshayla rep# 1010734 explained to me that even though I only had service for 10 days I had to pay for the entire month and didn't even offer to take off the late charge because she claimed I was texted the final bill which is bull because I never received said text and they don't prorate. Paying for 2/3 of a month that I did not have any service is a ridiculous so they can forget getting my money

I canceled service in October 2024. I called a few weeks later about a refund of the credit balance on my account. I was told that payment would be returned to my bank account after my final bill was generated on November 2. No refund arrived. It is now November 19 and every time I call, the story flips between "sending it to your bank" and "We're sending you a gift card". I still have no refund. Today, they told me my balance is zeroed out; there is nothing more they can do for me. No accountability and the left hand has no idea what the right hand is doing.

I noticed I was billed twice in March 
I jump on webchat 
The agent stuffs around for 15 mins
Makes me fill in a form with personal details 
I input my pin code, forms is submitted
Minutes later he says pin is wrong but can still help 
More time passes 
Then he tells me he can’t help without the pin!!!
Ffs - all these companies using useless shitty webchat companies as a fake assistance point need to go out of business. If u can’t give us customer service then stop pretending 
Wasted time and frustration is the only outcome from using crappy operators 
And to lead the customer on for 20 mins is disgraceful. Hope T-Mobile is happy with the fake stats of their agents and the alleged enquiries they handle! More like increasing the distrust and frustration of customers who need resolutions 
I should not have to waste an hour to find out why you double billed
I would give zero stars if I could

Three times I encountered this TMoble Rep from Westfield Topanga Mall, Los Angeles, Ca and the experience was just rude and traumatic.  The first experience was a roughly a year ago.  I came in the shop and he was standing by the door.  I inquired about upgrading my phone and he was impolite and sounded not interested to help me, he simply said, "ok choose your phone in the displays" and did not even offer to open my account.  I dismissed the experience and went out of the shop discourage about pursuing my intention to upgrade.  I never forget his face.  then almost a year later I went back with my wife to ask about the status of our account (how much we pay monthly, are we eligible for upgrade etc) the first thing he said to me was "you don't know how much you pay on your bill? sounding sarcastic and irritated that i requested him to open my account.  I let that experience go because i was tired and in a hurry.  the third encounter with AJ was today Nov 17, 2024.  Two hours ago. And it was horrible. We came in with the intention to upgrade our phones.  He opened our account and told us that we need to pay our overdue bill first.  We were surprised of the unpaid bill because we thought we are on auto debit.  But he said sarcastically "No you haven't paid last month's bill".  that what it means by "overdue" sounding like we don't understand what the word means.  So this finally into me and reacted with all the bottled feelings i have had in the previous two encounters.  I told him this is the 3rd he has been rude to us and he accused me of making up stories and of raising my voice. And he said he has the right to refuse service and told us to leave in front of all the customers.  He is a liability to your brand.  The lady representative who was beside us was not helpful either. I know the shop is closing for the day but that is no reason to be horrible to the customer.  This experience is not acceptable.

T Mobile updated their Voice Mail application, and it is no longer accessible.  Called the help desk, and they didn’t know what to do either.  The T Mobile new voice mail application used to work fine, however the new application is broken, and their folks do not know how to fix it yet.
There is no way in Hell I would do business with any company that associates themseives with Snoop Dogg!! What a piece of garbage.

I chose T-mobile after being treated like garbage by AT&T.  I chose T-mobile primary because they U.S. based support.  Now 2 years later, they've replace humans with typical Automated cuStomer abuSe (ASS) roadblocking system.  I've had to listen to it > 2 than a minute of canned frustrating announcements before it would put me into a queue for a human representative.  If you're lucky enough to get an American representative, they're still great; it's the greedy executive decisions to put up roadblocks to you talking to anyone.

No stars if allowable. They are liars and scammers. When communication with them on social media they’ll block you for leaving a negative review. They are the worst company. Charge you for bills after your number has been ported to another carrier. Reported this on my credit and refused to remove it after they agree it’s their error. I’ve already experienced adverse effects from this from long standing creditors.

Don't buy any accessories through t-mobile!  I have been with them for years but the return window is so short and you can't return anything without doing a live chat.  I spent days using the app and the online system and finally decided to chat with an agent.  It took several tries to get through the automated agent and then I received no help from the live agent.

They are definitely liars. Promise you one thing and do something different. It's impossible to talk to an English person let alone a manager. You can't reason with these foreigners. Best you can do is file a complaint with the FCC and Better business.

Left T-Mobile because they did the bait and switch regarding free Netflix. Now I have a credit balance for $(6.79) and they say they can't access my account without a pin to issue me a refund check. Instead I keep getting monthly statements saying my credit will be applied to next months bill. Worst customer service and a reminder why I left them.

I got my first cell phone in 1994, just when cell phones were becoming popular. My service then was through AT&T Wireless.  As you can imagine at that time cell towers were few and far between. My cell service then was 10 times better than it is today with T mobile.  T mobiles’ service is absolutely terrible everywhere I go!!   I’ll be loving as soon as possible and will NEVER return!

I was offered a service for moving all my business lines over by James Robertson of T-Mobile via email. None of that agreement played out the way it was supposed to. No one in support will accept an email to adjust bill. I cancelled all service immediately after being told there was no way to get my bill to what was offered.

I have been with T-mobile for many, many years the call quality is solid  however, the customer service is by far the most subpar, well below standard customer service I have ever dealt with agents and supervisors are apparently only allowed to stay on script and can in no way vary to provide actual customer service. The automated system is set up to ask you the same questions a minimum of 3 times each before it will transfer you to an agent and the agents make it clear that they will do no more if not less the what the automated system can do, and do not let me even get started on the procedures they make up and put in place that get you to go into the store where the store associates then look at you lost and say “I’m not sure why they told you to come in for that? We will have to call customer service!” An overseas agent set up my initial business account over the phone and completely misspelled my name. It to 2 years to partially clear this up and the random name now appears on my credit report. I sadly wish I was making this up lol. This is just on scenario.

This company does nothing but lie to customers constantly. I spent 8 months being promised working internet coverage and cell phone service at one end of town where it was non-existant. Instead of one month for them to fix the problem it took 9 months before they built another tower that actually worked. They also promised that T-Mobile had agreements with European countries so that my plan included internet coverage in Europe. That was a blatant lie. Out of 8 cities -- it only worked in Vienna. I bought trackers from them because they promised there would be no monthly fee. That was a lie. They started charging the monthly fees after the return window had closed and then refused to accept a return of the sealed boxes and unused trackers. After I finally cancelled all my services with T-Mobile they tried to charge me the full amount for the 4 trackers. When I disputed these charges and won the disputes with Citibank -- T-Mobile tried to bill me again for the full amount of the disputed charges and also threatened me with reporting the debt to credit agencies, even though they are bound by arbitration agreements to honor the dispute decisions in my favor by Citibank.

I don’t have a complaint about wireless service or coverage. I do have a huge complaint about today’s customer Service.  Customer service was not this bad 1 - 2 years ago. Now you call and they don’t listen to you. It’s like they’re told to say you owe them money. I told the last representative that I wanted to talk to his manager and he didn’t want to transfer that call. He kept telling me I owe them money and there’s no need to talk to anyone else. It’s so frustrating and disappointing that T-Mobile hires unprofessional people and please T-Mobile hire people who speaks decent English. I’m done with T-Mobile!!

Absolute worst service and company to deal with. I don't recommend them to anyone. Not only do they lie about products and billing, they don't honor anything they say. We canceled service within 2 weeks, Was told we had 30 days. T-mobile sent us a label to return and said it's within timeframe. We got a bill for $2300 and they say there is nothing they will do about it. So they took returned phones and still billed us for them. Worst customer service ever!!!

T-Mobile really sucks. They gave me a price for two internets at $60 a month. Three people from the store said that a week later I get a bill from them for $90. I haven't even done my trial. And they're already charging me. So I go back in the store to say it's supposed to be only $60. Will I get a different guy? And he says oh those people were misinformed. They didn't know the sale went off so they wouldn't honor it. So I brought my stuff back before I even finished my trial.. called them to get my $90 back and you have to have a PIN number so I had to go back to the store and figure out what my PIN number was. Finally a month later they send me out a statement saying that I have a credit of $52 which is BS. So I call him to tell him no. You owe me $90. They can't help me because I need a PIN number which I don't have which I don't know. So they can't help me at all. I have to go back to the store to get a PIN number. If you're thinking about using T-Mobile don't please don't

T-Mobile in person has mostly helpful people, but many things need to be done online or on the phone.  The 611 phone customer service people are so incredibly inept it is 😤😡. I just sat on the phone for 2 hours and they could not set up a simple account for me. They kept putting me on hold without explaining what the problem was. When they asked for my social, I told the woman I had been a victim of identity theft, so I would have to get off the phone with her and release the freeze on my credit account. She said no need, they just needed my social on file. I sat for another hour on the phone before someone in the credit department came on to say I needed to release the freeze on my credit. I said "I told the other woman that an hour ago!" What is wrong with you? Do your people not know what their job is? Why does the person setting up the account NOT know what she is asking?!

They are only nice as long as your a customer, but as soon as you switch then thier attuide changes when you talk to um on the phone. They over charge you on your bill and use a phrase called a one time charge to make money after you canceled your service to charge you  7 dollars over and over again

Promised excellent coverage in the neighborhood by my house and it turned out to be dropped calls all the time.  I spent a crazy amount of time with customer service and they have done nothing except try to get me to convince the church in my neighborhood to put a t mobile tower on their roof.  Really mobile, it that my job our yours?!

Terrible service/coverage. Did not use the entirely of the last month’s billing cycle before switching to another company, charged extras fees vs getting back what was unused. Guess ya gotta keep yourself in business somehow. 🤷‍♀️  the 1-2 star average reviews don’t lie.

I have my sons phone plan payments linked to my checking account and it’s on auto pay for the 29th of every month and evidently it did not come out due to we were homeless at the time and they went into my account on November 8 and just took out the payment without notifying me first which is illegal it’s unauthorized and its theft when I called the girl we just went round in circles and I hung up on her. She called back. I told her to give my money back or report you to authorities that she took out money Out of my account unauthorized and it’s theft!
Had trouble from the very beginning. Overtime the service got worse and worse. Finally cut it off today. We have been without internet for days now.

Customer service is a total Joke

The 5G service is great but the app and support are embarrassing bad.

T-Mobile uses deceptive promotions, by saying you have to be targeted to qualify while at the same time trying to get you to change plans when in the detail description of the promotions never mentions anything about having to be targeted, I am a customer of over 20 years dating back to Sprint

Do not go for Tmobile, they literally scam you for money. I ordered for a T mobile hotspot, but I received a TCL tablet with sim card. When I called customer service, I was told that fake account with pin was already crerated, I have to go to a nearby t mobile store, I requested for cancellation of my account and autopay. I returned back the equipment within a  month. They have cancelled my account but not the autopay. I got a mail that I have to pay for TCL tablet, eventhough I returned it. Now I am being told that I have to go to near by T mobile store to open my closed account and ask for refund. This is my more than 6 times calling customer service and three times going to T mobile store in a month.

When you need clear answers they are of no help and leave you on your own. Will never be a customer again. 
I was a long time customer of T-Mobile, almost 15 years. 
In September of 2024, a promotion was presented to me, I accepted as it was a great deal. After the fact,T-Mobile  told me I was not eligible for the promotion and canceled the order on their side after the new phone had shipped and I was charged. I took my business elsewhere and have no regrets! Should have done it years ago. 
Settling up with T-Mobile for the refund owed to me and the charges due about when my account ended etc. have been much more than a headache. Going on 2 months of dealing with these people (over the phone, in-person and online).
I left t mobile for spectrum, and they hit me with a $173 bill after I had already paid their "final bill" at the time I switched carriers.  Total scam!!!

Total scam if you are a veteran. They promise Vets a better monthly price to lure them in. Then they offer a ridiculous trade in offer that makes the bill higher than if you don't qualify for a discount.

Oh my, where to start? Just for them to install the internet (confirmation of the time and date) and for me to register my account, I had to contact them repeatedly for 2-3 days, like 10-15 times. NO support received "in the system it seem right", "I cannot guarantee", "you have to download the APP to register an online account", "we don't know how to register an acoount from the browser" etc... what even was that? never have i ever seen a company so incompetent

Terrible customer service. Utterly incompetent team who messed up my sim and I had to go a week without a phone service because they cancelled my sim and refused to reactivate it so that I can port out. They also took 60 dollars without my permission and without checking compatibility with my phone. It took them 2 seconds to take the money and then cancel my sim. but 5 days before anyone started on working on my issue. they also lied to me and 2 hrs per day on call with they horrible care reps

I was talking with a customer service manager about resolving a bill issue and they were going to help out if I made a specific payment which I did and they were going to follow up the next business day to finish the agreement by that time they went and deducted the original payment which ended up to 460 total and been trying to rectify the situation but not getting anywhere no matter who I speak with in customer service they all basically say we don't care.
Very bad services. Extra charges that are not suppose to be there.
They took 1731.96 out of my account for phones I sent back. The shipping labels proved they received them and they still charged me and refused to give me a refund.

Horrible idea for businesses. They will upsell you on promotional lines to reduce your monthly bill but then not close the lines out and continue to charge you for them when you move carriers because their reception is terrible. Avoid, avoid, avoid.

We got a hot spot for our camp ground and it didn’t work at all. Spent time on the phone with  a person trying to troubleshoot our issue.  The issue was the hot spot wasn’t strong enough for our area.  We had an emergency and needed the WiFi and we were out of luck.  So the very next day. We went to a store and got a portable WIFI.  Which has served us well.  So now we have a hot spot that we want to return and they are making us pay for something that doesn’t work for a total of 2 years. I think this is horrible customer service.  We have our cell phone service through them and they do t really care. They are still making us be responsible for this bill. We are retired and can’t afford to pay for something that doesn’t work.  I am so frustrated with the service that we have received. What are we supposed to do?

i brought a samsang s24plus DO NOT BUY THIS PHONE this is the worst phone i have ever had i have been a tmobile customer for 22 years and they want to charge me $40.00 to return the phone that i have had for only 2 days....please make sure you listen to these reviews i should have

I often find it challenging to connect with the new generation, as our perspectives differ significantly. Nevertheless, I had a positive experience with Niko and Kimberly, employees at the T-Mobile store located at 2605 Woodland Blvd, Deland, while upgrading my iPhone from a 12 to a 16. Lacking familiarity with the latest smartphones, I was grateful for Kimberly's exceptional patience and understanding. Throughout the phone transfer process, both Niko and Kimberly kept me engaged and informed about T-Mobile's current options and promotions. They took the time to review my account and provided a brief tutorial on the new features of the iPhone 16, even after store hours, without making me feel rushed. Their professionalism and support made a significant difference, and I truly appreciate their contributions to T-Mobile.

It's ridiculous that their system requires I remember account number when I have been using online services only.IT IS ABSOLUTELY ABSURD  and aggravating that I can't even contact assistance through the chat without remembering the account number.

I’ve used T-Mobile military Business for twenty-five years. A few problems. One was never fixed. Our son went overseas. Now when people call my number a message says “Put these numbers into your phone to complete the call” I gave up on the fix WHAT HAS MADE ME ANGERY IS GIANT DOWN PAYMENTS. I have perfect credit. I’m disappointed in T-Mobile.

T-Mobile states that once you agree to their plan, you bill will never go up. Ok. Sounds great!! Count me in!! Uh, except that is not true. My first bill was higher and the second bill was higher than that!! After spending hours on the phone with customer service, "we can't do anything. The store manager will have to make those changes". The store manager quoted me a price that was the correct price, but the bill is more. Oh guess what? Since you've been trying to get this problem fixed, "we've suspended your service". Now that's how you treat a new customer!! Lie to them. Charge them more than they've agreed to, then, cut them off when they ask what about the "you bill will never go up". Outstanding. No wonder they don't check trustpilot and respond to their reviews. They know exactly what they are doing. As always, should've checked trust pilot before committing. Lesson learned.

cell phone reception has been AMAZING...all my calls and messages come through no problem I have no problem when paying my bill,customer service is always prompt upfront and always helpful I joined t-mobile January of this year right after this year started before that I was with Verizon for almost 13 yrs and I couldn't wait to leave them considering I was already having issues right before I left and I wouldn't leave t-mobile now if somebody paid me I have nothing bad or negative to say about them unlike everybody else who talks nonsense about tmobile everyday it's just uncalled for but I never have ANY issues anywhere it's all smooth sailing and i'm not leaving them anytime soon for the foreseeable future I'm happy with their service much better than Verizon also their home wifi is pretty good too very fast speeds tmobile has impressed me in every sense of the way,this is my 100 percent honest review of t-mobile I'm a VERY happy customer

author.name: James Donald Donnie Adair Jr A

text: From the very 1st encounter setting up my account I've had nothing but the best customer service. I question everything yet whether I'm on the phone or f2f, they always answer my questions patiently and politely. The customer service reps can "see" everything in my account and can address all aspects. I moved from another big mobile company that made my every move to leave as painful as possible. T-mobile helped me and even adjusted my account because I missed a deadline to take advantage of the discounts for moving to T-mobile. I've even used the chat feature (which is traditionally like a torture chamber) and found the people on the chat side actually competent and able to handle my service needs. I've been a customer for quite a while and can say T-mobile has come a LONG WAY in service quality.

T-Mobile used a bait and switch tactic to get me locked into their service. They talked me into trading two paid and unlocked iPhones for new iPhones 16s “at no cost” if I stayed with them. But they charged me anyway and claimed they made a mistake. As a senior citizen I filed a complaint with my states attorney general. Still, as of now, I’m on the hook for $1600. Run from this company

Horrible customer service. I was a 20 year customer and the past couple of years the customer service has taken a nose dive. After T-Mobile customer service kept messing with my account because autopay failed to work, I was locked out of my account without a PIN and there after customer service would not help me since I had no PIN. 

I ported my numbers out to another company and T-Mobile continued to make it difficult to even pay my last bill. I paid my final bill using the web page address in the final bill letter to pay as guest. I thought I was finally done but that was not the case. 

I ended up getting a call from a collection agent stating I owe T-Mobile for the final bill. I provided REF# from my payment and I still could not get it resolved. 

I ended up filling a complaint with FCC and got an email from T-Mobile Executive Response Team. I called the ERT member as asked in the email and we finally spoke. ERT argued with me telling that the account was not in collection and disagreed with me about being called by a collection agent. He was able to locate payment and apply to my final bill. But even though the issue was resolved the customer service was still bad and only got resolution since it was a FCC complaint.

The instore sales people are wholly incompetent and then fall back on the signed paperwork as what is confirmed.

Even if the money to tmobile is exactly the same, they won't rectify anything on their end and will fall back on the contract terms.

Woefully incompetent through and through sales/customer service reps and frankly should be a class action lawsuit somewhere.

If I could leave a zero rating I would.  I was with them for nearly 25 years with always paying them on time.  However switching from their company to another is hell with misinformation and multiple late fees as a result.  I advise if you are starting with a new cell phone carrier to choose any other company.

I used their $50 a month internet service for around two years and it was constant problems.  I'd attempt to get support through the app and it would just report everything was fine and connected when it clearly wasn't.  I could never get a real person to respond.  When I switched to AT&T Fiber it took me six weeks to get my T-Mobile cancelled; they would respond to me and then stop replying when I held my ground about cancelling.  I finally had to take the box into a local store and they said THEY couldn't even help me cancel, but had to call a national number.  I showed them the screenshots of conversations showing I tried to cancel and asked if I could get a refund and they denied.  Then I got a bill showing they had sent my account immediately to collections for the month after I stopped using the service, two weeks into trying to cancel (I had turned off autopay, the one thing I COULD do).

Once we got our month T Mobile SIM installed it worked well, no problem there.  However, the SIM was sold as a month of use in the USA, when in fact service was terminated before midday on the 30th day, so effectively we only got 29 days use.  This was even more annoying as it meant we had no means of communicating on our day of departure.  Since when was a month only 29 days???

Stay away from them.  Spent all day on phone with Customer service over a bill and told nothing they can do switched to them cause we're cheaper than Verizon but guess you get what you pay for they don't value you only how much money they can get out of you

I have been charged, on my credit card, for 10 months for mobile hotspot service after leaving T-Mobile for a competitor.  They "can't" stop charging me over the phone.  I have to go into a retail location.  Would be easier to just shut down the credit card.

I’ve had T Mobile for about a year and half now.
They made an arrangement with me to pay a bill but they made the arrangement for back to back days and I told them that I could only afford the one payment. I made the payment but they shut off my service. After discussing the matter with them I made another arrangement and they promised not to turn the service off They turned it off twice in less than two weeks. They did this after promising that they wouldn’t. The rep then lied to me even though I have the text messages from 611
I will never buy another t mobile phone!!!! I have been with them for the last 10 years, and put up with the spotty cellphone service, because it was a little cheaper family plan.

I was with Tmobile for over 20 years. The only payment I missed was after Hurricane Wilma in like 2004. I didn't have service that entire month. It all started in June when I returned a old Samsung Flip phone 3. The Screensaver separates at the fold. It is my 3rd one. They sent me a refurbished one. When I sent it back on time!!!! I saved the receipt it was never scanned at the warehouse. Long story short, 5 months later, 12 hours on the phone and 2 trips to the store. They constantly took out and put in $1100 and $2200 out of an account that I only use to pay bills. Not only does it come out. It is constantly "pending". So I have multiple times it goes in and out. So I call all the time. I was very nice in the beginning. Wells Fargo told me to immediately take them off bill pay. After it was finally settled. It happened again!!! My 0 balance went to $1279 because they kept hitting me with late fees. The customer service rep said I should have had the money to cover the $1100 in good faith. 17× my $65 bill. Now I have $2200 in my Wells Fargo account that they can't take out!!! I called them and let them know that I have their money. The lady got me so mad. I went to Patriot Mobile. I am waiting on the SIM card. I called one more time to talk to a supervisor. Another 44 minutes on hold. So now 12 hours. He said he was the number one guy in the entire USA at Tmobile. He said it would be 3-5 days until he EMAILS his team. I said you are a phone company and you can't call them? I said you don't understand...I am not going to be your customer in 5 days! They don't give a crap!! It is a bunch of kids and foreigners. They don't care about the company. They can't deal with a 61 year old retired buisinessman who doesn't tolerate foolishness. Find another company.

They said we would get 4 new phones for switching.  Guess what older phones aren't eligible for trade in.  Coverage area stinks in our area.  

Too bad we switched from Verizon.
I have known T-Mobile for 25 years and it is thrust worthy company.

This is the the worst app I am so sorry I got involved with T-Mobile home Internet.  

It is constantly getting disconnected. I'm trying to watch a movie and all of a sudden it starts filtering you can't stay connected long enough to watch a full movie. 

Their Wi-Fi is horrible I went out today using directions I get halfway there and I'm stranded because their T-Mobile Wi-Fi just disappeared and I had nothing I didn't know where I was going they weren't even on my list of connections, they completely disappeared off my phone.

I was only 10 minutes away from my home and they're saying that there wi-fi can only go so far, this is ridiculous. They are the worst worst app for internet. Like I said there's not a program I can watch fully without being disconnected.

I'm trying to surf the Internet or play some word games you can't do it because it's so slow you get so frustrated you give up.

Consider removing autopay and e-billing at least 2 months before changing your plan or canceling service. I canceled all service on April 3 and after 6 phone calls, and 3 calls to T-Mobile Executive Office, I received my refund on October 22. The $10 reduction every month on my phone bill was not worth 29 weeks of agony and false promises after my account was closed.

Have been with this company for a while and always paid my bill. When one of my family members who is on my account died they wouldn't even extend my due date. So that I could pay and attend the funeral. Just so they could charge me and additional 80.00 in late fees. This company is all about thier money not their customers. They simply do not care.

T-Mobile is a complete damn scam. I paid the bill. That’s why I got rid of my phone. I didn’t like the service I’m on the phone talking to it’s  cut off. I paid the bill. They bill me for $1.25 keep adding me fees for Phone that I don’t have. That’s why I got rid of the complete scam.

I’ve been with T Mobil for 7 years and this is the worst I’ve been treated. 
I bought an iPhone 16 and paid the taxes of $97.71 online, because the phone was not available in the store. When I reported it and an exchange was being made, first I had to pay $97.71 AGAIN! Now they are saying it’s non refundable? After staying on the phone with someone from a foreign country, for over an house, I’m getting a refund but some will be refunded to my account, and the other to my card! Plus they took the money of mine without any problem, but I have to wait a month to get MY MONEY refunded. I’m annoyed, angry, and disappointed! This is a bad way to do business!
They make so much profit, but why is it impossible to make a good website for refilling your prepaid card???? Never again

Worst experience, Becareful , they are only nice when you sign in and join after no service .. whatsoever 
Bad connection, bad service, they are only care to charge you 
Worst overseas service but still they charge you fully Aldo you could barely can get service, 
Rude and unprofessional costumer service or better say no costumer service or help at all. 
When you call the don’t give a damn about you. 
They look for excuse to hang up on you 
They do not keep their promises if you request for something. 
Just 
Worst phone company in USA

Would give 0 stars if it were an option, Tmobile has overpriced plans. I would recommend basically any other cell service company if you care about what is in your bank account. If you do have Tmobile currently, better watch the amount closely because it can and will change on you with no notice beyond just being present on your billing statement. If you're canceling your bill, you better ask if there are any additional charges because if there is, they will not inform you beyond a letter in the mail like we in the 1960s and they don't have your email address.

I have not received my rebates!  I have reached out a total of 5 times-months later no rebate!  I will be calling corporate tomorrow, followed up by a call to the BBB.  Nothing but lies, deception, aggravation and anxiety dealing with T-mobile!  You call and the call gets directed to the Philippines.  Second, i specifically asked to speak to the “retention team”.  I asked them “why should I stay with T-Mobile?  I switched carriers AND bundled home internet.  There was an incentive of a total of 300$ in rebates.  Thats not true, I have not received my rebate incentives.  The day I switched carriers verizon offered home wifi and for less money than t-mobile.   So please tell me why should I stay with T-Mobile?  Your job is a retention specialist tell me…why should i stay?” (Anne) listed a bunch of services ATT, VERIZON and many other competitors offer.  She discussed my bill that was due 2 days ago.  I explained “I was aware of my obligations, and told her that they did not answer my question! “.  Now they got a supervisor on the phone.  He was about as useful as a pair of tits on a bull!  Regurgitated the same bs as the previous person.  I asked him the very specific question…”why should I continue to be a T-mobile customer?  He went on about the services and i told him (Dave) your competitors offer that too and for less money…

The retention team did not answer the question and failed at their task.  
i was with ATT cellular for 20 years (and probably another 20 before that with the old landline!  I left them, because I did not feel valued.  (New customers were getting better deals than myself and i had to ask for comparable discounts.)  i was with verizon for 3 years and switched for the incentives.  Like i mentioned earlier the day i switched they came out with a promo for less money in a home internet plan than what i was NEWLY paying at t-mobile.  Now no rebates?  Yes I will be reaching out to Bbb, corporate, and bringing this to the attention of some litigious lawyers.  Bring on the class action suit!

WTG- Mike Seivert!
